meaning: v.侥幸成功,意外受挫n.锚爪,意外的挫折,侥幸,倒霉
examples: The discovery was something of a fluke.
They are determined to show that their last win was no fluke.
The whale retained a tail and lacked a fluke, the major means of locomotion in modern cetaceans.
As recently as a year ago people wondered whether M-PESA's success was a fluke.
During this time, though, almost as a fluke, DeRose discovered an ability to sing.
